page: Microsoft Azure E96-24s_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Esv6 family. It is equipped with 24 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz, featuring 12 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The system is configured with 768.0 GB of memory, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate strong memory performance, with large-block read-only memory bandwidth reaching 187.67 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 116.85 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% of tested servers. Single-core performance is also notable, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2142.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 31.14 GB/sec. Multi-core and database operations perform in the average range, with a Passmark CPU score of 37633.32 and Redis SET operations achieving 35.07 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E96-24s_v6 offers high memory density relative to its compute resources, making it a specialized choice for memory-bound workloads rather than raw compute-heavy tasks. The lack of bundled local storage means users must provision external Premium Storage, which represents a key architectural tradeoff. However, the high-capacity L3 cache of 260 MB and advanced vector and matrix extensions, such as AVX-512 and AMX, provide efficient processing for specialized vector and integer operations.

This server is highly suited for memory-intensive applications. Appropriate workloads include large-scale in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, enterprise relational databases, and data analytics workloads that require massive RAM capacity without a proportional increase in CPU licensing costs.
description: The Microsoft Azure E96-24s_v6 is a memory-optimized server from the Esv6 family, featuring 24 dedicated vCPUs and 768.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz, this x86_64 virtual machine delivers 32.0 GB of memory per core. While it lacks local storage and GPUs, it is Premium Storage capable and runs on Microsoft Hyper-V. Benchmarks reveal strong memory bandwidth, with large-block read speeds of 187.67 GB/sec and write speeds of 116.85 GB/sec. Single-core performance is also robust, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2142.0. Conversely, multi-core and database operations, such as Redis SET operations at 35.07 million operations per second, align with average tiers. This configuration is highly efficient for memory-bound workloads, making it an appropriate fit for in-memory databases, large caching layers, and data analytics.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96-24s_v6 is a memory-optimized server featuring 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB of RAM, and strong memory bandwidth, making it ideal for in-memory databases and caching.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96-24s_v6 is a memory-optimized server with 24 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and strong large-block memory bandwidth.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring high RAM-to-CPU density and strong memory bandwidth for demanding in-memory database workloads.
bullet_points: 24 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz
768.0 GB of RAM providing an ultra-high 32.0 GB per core ratio
Strong large-block memory read bandwidth of 187.67 GB/sec
Premium Storage capable with no local storage bundled
Best fit for in-memory databases, caching layers, and data analytics
